categories.title: Emotional Fine-Grained Control
categories.description: Starting from the same initial utterance, the Refiner re-synthesizes the speech to simultaneously convey the target emotion and apply a specified word-level prosodic edit (stress or pause insertion). Each row shares the same emotion; each column applies a different fine-grained control.
categories.text: Listen closely, the same words can tell a very different story.

categories.title: Speed & Pitch Control
categories.description: Beyond emotion and word-level edits, the Refiner also controls global prosodic attributes. From the same initial utterance, it produces 9 variants spanning 3 speed levels and 3 pitch levels while consistently applying the specified stress and pause.
categories.text: Listen closely, the same words can tell a very different story.
categories.instruction: Speak at a {speed} speed and {pitch} pitch. Specifically, insert a pause after 'words' and stress the word 'very'.

categories.title: Refinement with Instruction
categories.description: In the full LoopTTS pipeline, the AudioLLM Judge listens to the initial utterance, diagnoses prosodic defects, and automatically generates a structured refine instruction that may specify emotion, word-level stress/pause, speed, and pitch corrections. The Refiner then executes the instruction to produce the corrected speech while preserving speaker identity. Below are real Judge-generated instructions and the corresponding before/after pairs.

samples.text: The car door slammed shut, punctuating the end of the argument.
samples.instruction: Speak with a Angry emotion. Specifically, stress the word 'slammed', stress the word 'shut', and stress the word 'end', insert a pause after 'end'.

samples.text: Do you really believe your silence is more tolerable than an honest mistake?
samples.instruction: Speak with a Angry emotion at a fast speed and moderate pitch. Specifically, stress the word 'really', stress the word 'silence', and stress the word 'honest'.

samples.text: You expect me to eat this after seeing how it's prepared?
samples.instruction: Speak with a Disgusted emotion at a slow speed and low pitch. Specifically, stress the word 'You', stress the word 'this', insert a pause after 'this', and stress the word 'prepared'.

samples.text: I've told you 100 times—don't open the door at night.
samples.instruction: Speak with a Fearful emotion. Specifically, stress the word '100', insert a pause after 'times', stress the word 'don't', and stress the word 'night'.

samples.text: I—i swear i wasn't alone down there! You have to believe me this time.
samples.instruction: Speak with a Fearful emotion at a moderate speed and low pitch. Specifically, stress the word 'swear', insert a pause after 'swear', stress the word 'wasn't', insert a pause after 'there!', and stress the word 'believe'.

samples.text: You've got to see this—it's even better than we imagined!
samples.instruction: Speak with a Happy emotion. Specifically, stress the word 'got', insert a pause after 'this', stress the word 'even', and stress the word 'imagined'.

samples.text: The letter slipped from his grasp, a paper cut of bitter news.
samples.instruction: Speak with a Sad emotion at a low pitch. Specifically, stress the word 'slipped', insert a pause after 'grasp', and stress the word 'bitter'.

samples.text: I know i messed up, but please don't leave—please.
samples.instruction: Speak with a Sad emotion at a slow speed and moderate pitch. Specifically, stress the word 'i', insert a pause after 'up', stress the word 'please', and insert a pause after 'leave'.

samples.text: Upon trying her hand at painting, she discovered a natural talent within her.
samples.instruction: Speak with a Surprised emotion at a slow speed. Specifically, stress the word 'painting', insert a pause after 'painting', stress the word 'natural', and stress the word 'talent'.

samples.text: I don't just understand you, Anne,' he said hesitatingly. 'You appear to be vexed about something.
samples.instruction: Speak at a fast speed. Specifically, stress the word 'understand', stress the word 'vexed', and insert a pause after 'Anne'.
